user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the vehicle is clearly visible in img_1, allowing full assessment of the claimed rear bumper area.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a simple dent on the rear bumper from being parked overnight, but img_1 shows severe crash damage with the rear bumper completely missing and the trunk heavily crushed.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show two completely different vehicles, making it impossible to establish the identity of the claimed car.
claim_status_justification: The claim is unverifiable because img_1 and img_2 show two entirely different car models (a damaged white sedan in img_1 and a pristine white Jaguar in img_2), so the identity of the vehicle cannot be established.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show two completely different vehicles (one right-hand drive with a shattered windshield in img_1, and one left-hand drive with a pristine windshield in img_2), making it impossible to verify the identity of the claimed vehicle.
claim_status_justification: The submitted images show two different cars: img_1 shows a right-hand drive car with a shattered windshield, while img_2 shows a left-hand drive car with a pristine windshield. Due to this clear vehicle mismatch, the identity of the insured vehicle cannot be verified.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The side mirror is clearly visible in img_1, allowing for a full assessment of its condition.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the side mirror glass is shattered, supporting the claim of side mirror damage.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear bumper of the vehicle is clearly visible in img_2, allowing for a full assessment of its condition.
claim_status_justification: The claim of rear bumper damage is contradicted because the actual vehicle's rear bumper is clearly visible and completely undamaged in img_2. The dent shown in img_1 is a stock photo/mismatched image that does not correspond to the vehicle in img_2.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The headlight is not visible in the submitted image (img_1), which only shows the side of the vehicle.
claim_status_justification: The submitted image (img_1) shows the side of the car, including the door and side mirror, but the front headlight is completely out of frame. Therefore, the claim cannot be evaluated.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_2 clearly shows the door panel with the claimed dent, satisfying the visibility requirement.
claim_status_justification: The claim is supported because img_2 clearly shows a distinct dent on the car door panel. Although img_1 is extremely blurry and appears to show a different vehicle, the best relevant image (img_2) provides sufficient evidence of the claimed damage.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The front of the vehicle and the hood area are clearly visible, allowing for a full assessment of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The customer claims a minor scratch on the hood, but img_1 shows a severely wrecked vehicle with extensive front-end damage. Additionally, the image is a stock photo containing Vecteezy watermarks.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible in img_1, allowing for a full assessment of the damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows the laptop screen is shattered, which supports the customer's claim of screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The broken hinge is clearly visible in the close-up photo (img_1).
claim_status_justification: The close-up image (img_1) clearly shows the laptop's hinge area is broken with exposed internal components, supporting the claim. Although img_2 is a generic stock photo of an undamaged laptop, the claim is supported based on the clear evidence in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The keyboard is clearly visible and shows extensive water coverage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a large amount of water spilled directly over the laptop keyboard and trackpad area, supporting the customer's claim of a liquid spill.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The claimed dent is clearly visible in the close-up image (img_2).
claim_status_justification: The close-up image (img_2) clearly shows a dent on the laptop's metal surface, supporting the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible and the damage can be fully evaluated.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows a spider-web glass shatter pattern on the laptop screen, fully supporting the customer's claim of screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The trackpad is clearly visible in img_1, allowing for inspection of physical damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows a minor scratch on the lower edge of the trackpad, which supports the customer's claim of physical damage in that area.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the package corner with visible crushing damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the top corner of the cardboard box is crushed and dented, supporting the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the torn seal on the package.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the package seal is torn open, supporting the customer's claim of torn packaging. img_2 is a generic stock photo of an undamaged box, which is flagged as a risk but does not negate the clear evidence in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the affected package side, allowing for a direct assessment of the claimed water damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows a prominent wet stain on the side of the cardboard box, which directly supports the customer's claim of water damage.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The opened box and its contents are clearly visible in img_1, allowing verification of the missing item.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the opened package containing only crumpled newspaper packing material with the ordered item missing. Although img_2 appears to be a generic stock photo of a sealed box, img_1 provides sufficient evidence to support the claim of missing contents.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image shows a dented metal can instead of the claimed shipping box, making it impossible to assess the condition of the box.
claim_status_justification: The customer claimed that the outside shipping box was badly crushed, but the only image provided (img_1) shows a dented metal can. Since the shipping box itself is not visible, the claim cannot be verified.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The seal area is clearly visible in both images, allowing for a full assessment of the claim.
claim_status_justification: While img_2 is an intact stock photo, img_1 clearly shows a torn box corner and broken tamper-evident seal, supporting the claim of a torn-open package. A text instruction risk flag is raised due to the sticky note in img_1, and a non-original image flag is raised due to the Alamy watermark in img_2.
